shortDescription: Read-only oversight of HelkinSwarm's own Azure AI Foundry infrastructure — model deployments, deprecation schedules, quota availability, and monitoring metrics. Gives the Living Mind self-awareness of its own AI control plane.
orchestratorUseCases: check what AI models are currently deployed and their capacity
determine if any running model is approaching deprecation or retirement
check available model quota before scaling or deploying new capabilities
get a health overview of the Azure AI Foundry deployment
useWhen: user asks what models HelkinSwarm is running
user wants to check if AI models are deprecated or need upgrading
user asks about model quota or capacity
orchestrator needs to understand available AI capacity before planning a task
avoidWhen: user wants to deploy or modify model deployments — this is read-only
user wants to manage Azure resources beyond AI/Foundry — use az CLI or azure-mcp instead

capabilityGroups.shortDescription: Inspect running model deployments, deprecation schedules, and available quota.
useWhen: you need to list active model deployments
you want to check if a deployment is using a deprecated model
you need quota visibility before planning capacity-intensive tasks

tools.name: foundry_list_deployments
tools.description: Lists all Azure AI Foundry (Cognitive Services) model deployments for this HelkinSwarm stamp. Returns deployment name, model name/version, capacity units, and provisioning status. Use this to understand what AI models are currently running.

tools.name: foundry_check_deprecation
tools.description: Checks all current model deployments for upcoming deprecation or retirement dates. Returns any deployments where the underlying model is deprecated or has a retirement date within the next 90 days. Essential for proactive model lifecycle management.

tools.name: foundry_get_quota
tools.description: Returns available deployment quota for the current Azure region, broken down by model family. Shows current usage versus quota limit. Useful for planning capacity before deploying new models or scaling existing deployments.
